HOME ESTABLISHMENT.INDIAN TROOP SERVICE vid EGYPT.

The business of the Troop Service is under the superintendence of Rear-Admiral H. W. Brent, R.N., Director of Transports at the Admiralty.
Consulting Officer for the Indian Troop Service.—Captain A. W. Chitty, late I.N.
Agent for Indian Troop Service at Suez.—Capt. W. Aves, R.I.M.
Resident Transport Officer (Bombay).—Capt. J. Hext, R.N., C.I.E.

ROYAL INDIAN ENGINEERING COLLEGE, COOPERS HILL.BOARD OF VISITORS.CHAIRMAN.

Sir Richard Temple, Bart., M.P., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., D.C.L.

MEMBERS.

W. H. Barlow, Esq., F.R.S., Past President, Institution of Civil Engineers.
Sir Dietrich Brandis, K.C.I.E., F.R.S., Ph.D.
Lieut.-Gen. C. H. Dickens, C.S.I., R.A.
Sir J. Fowler, K.C.M.G., Past President, Institution of Civil Engineers.
W. H. Preece, Esq., F.R.S., Member of Council, Institution of Civil Engineers.
Lieut.-Gen. R. Strachey, C.S.I., R.E., F.R.S.
Sir Alexander M. Rendel, K.C.I.E., M.A., Member of Institution of Civil Engineers.
Hugh Leonard, Esq., Late Chief Engineer, Indian Public Works Department; Fellow and President of the Faculty of Engineering, Calcutta University.

STAFF OF THE COLLEGE.

President.—Gen. Sir A. Taylor, G.C.B., R.E.

Secretary.—Lt.-Col. W. J. Boyes

Bursar.—J. P. Pasco, Esq., R.N., Retired.

Chaplain.—Rev. C. Crosleigh, D.D.

Medical Officer.—H. E. Giffard, Esq.

ENGINEERING AND APPLIED MECHANICS BRANCHES.

Professor of Engineering Construction.—Callcott Reilly, Esq., M.I.C.E.

Professor of Hydraulic Engineering and Mechanism.—T. A. Hearson, Esq., M.I.C.E.

Assistant Professor of Engineering.—A. H. Heath, Esq., Assoc. Inst. C.E.

Professor of Surveying.—Maj.-Gen. E. H. Courtney, R.E.

Instructor in Surveying.—Captain J. Stewart, R.E.

Instructor in Geometrical Drawing and in Estimating, and Lecturer in Architecture.—A. Hicks, Esq.

Assist. Instr. in Geometrical Drawing.—C. B. McElwee, Esq.

Lecturer in Accounts.—J. C. Hurst, Esq.

Demonstrator in the Mechanical Laboratory.—Philip Reilly, Esq.

MATHEMATICAL BRANCH.

Professor of Mathematics.—G. M. Minchin, Esq., M.A.

Professor of Pure Mathematics.—A. Lodge, Esq., M.A., Fereday Fellow of St. John's College, Oxon.

NATURAL SCIENCE BRANCH.

Professor of Chemistry.—Herbert McLeod, Esq., F.R.S.

Demonstrator in Chemistry.—F. E. Matthews, Esq., Ph.D., F.I.C.

Analytical Chemist.—F. W. Harbord, Assoc. Royal School of Mines, F.I.C.

Professor of Physics.—W. N. Stocker, Esq., M.A., Fellow of Brasenose College, Oxon.

Demonstrator in Physics.—T. Shields, Esq.

Lecturer in Geology and Mineralogy.—Professor H. G. Seeley, F.R.S.

FORESTRY BRANCH.

Principal Professor of Forestry.—W. Schlich, Esq., C.I.E., Ph.D., F.L.S., late Inspector Genl. of Forests to Government of India.

Assistant Professor of Forestry.—W. R. Fisher, Esq., B.A., Conservator of Forests, Indian Forest Service.

Professor of Botany.—H. Marshall Ward, Esq., M.A., F.R.S., Fellow of Christ's College, Cambridge.

Lecturer on Entomology.—W. F. H. Blandford, Esq., M.A.

Lecturer on Organic Chemistry.—A. H. Church, Esq., M.A., F.R.S.

UNCLASSIFIED SUBJECTS.

Instructor in Freehand Drawing.—Russell Dowson, Esq.

Instructor in French.—J. A. Perret, Esq.

Instructor in German.—T. H. Dittel, Esq.

Director of the Practical Course of Forestry on the Continent.—Sir D. Brandis,* K.C.I.E., F.R.S., Ph. D., late Inspector Genl. of Forests to the Government of India.

* Sir D. Brandis is not on the Staff of the College

THE MOST EXALTED ORDER OF THE
STAR OF INDIA,†

INSTITUTED 1861. ENLARGED 1866, 1875, and 1876.

SOVEREIGN OF THE ORDER

THE QUEEN.

GRAND MASTER OF THE ORDER

{(His Excellency THE) MARQUESS OF LANSDOWNE, G.C.M.G., G.M.I.E.), the
VICEROY and GOVERNOR-GENERAL of INDIA for the time being.

Knights Grand Commanders. (18 Indian Chiefs, &c. 12 Europeans.)

THE MOST EMINENT
ORDER OF THE INDIAN EMPIRE.

INSTITUTED 1ST JANUARY, 1878, ENLARGED 1886, 1887, AND 1892.†

SOVEREIGN OF THE ORDER

The QUEEN.

GRAND MASTER OF THE ORDER.

The VICEROY and GOVERNOR-GENERAL of INDIA for the time being
(His Excellency the MARQUESS OF LANSDOWNE, G.M.S.I., G.C.M.G.).

IMPERIAL ORDER OF THE CROWN OF INDIA.

INSTITUTED 1st JANUARY, 1878.

SOVEREIGN OF THE ORDER,—The QUEEN.

LADIES OF THE ORDER.—C.I.

GOVERNMENT OF INDIA.

VICEROY AND GOVERNOR-GENERAL OF INDIA, The Most Hon. The MARQUESS OF LANSDOWNE, G.M.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.M.I.E.

Took his seat 10th December, 1888.

PERSONAL STAFF OF THE GOVERNOR-GENERAL.

Private Secretary.—Col. J. C. Ardagh, C.B., C.I.E., R.E.

Asst. Private Secretary.—D. Panioty, C.I.E.

Military Secretary.—Col. Lord W. De la P. Beresford, V.C., C.I.E.

Aides-de-Camp.

Capt. Hon. C. Harbord, Scots Gds.

Lt. H. A. Pakenham, Grn. Gds.

Lt. G. P. Brasier-Creagh, 9th Bengal Lancers

Capt. L. Herbert, S.C., C. I. Horse

Extra Aides-de-Camp.

Lt. S. H. Pollen, Wiltshire Regt.

Capt. P. A. H. Viscount Valletort, 3rd Batt. Duke of Cornwall's L. I.

Native Aides-de-Camp.

Subadar Shaik Imdad Ali, Bahadur, Body Guard

Subadar-Major Tara Singh, Sirdar Bahadur, 45 Bengal N.I.

Surgeon.

Surg.-Lt.-Col. E. H. Fenn, Grn. Gds.

COUNCIL.

Extraordinary Member, Gen. Lord Roberts, G.C.B., G.C.I.E., V.C., R.A., Commander-in-Chief. Took his seat 28th November, 1885

Ordinary Members.

Sir Philip P. Hutchins, K.C.S.I. Took his seat 17th November, 1888

Sir David M. Barbour, K.C.S.I. Took his seat 27th November, 1888

Sir Alexander E. Miller, Q.C. Took his seat 8th April 1891

Lt.-Gen. Henry Brackenbury, C.B., R.A. Took his seat 25th April, 1891

Sir Charles Bradley Pritchard, K.C.I.E., C.S.I. Took his seat 29th November, 1892

Extraordinary Members, The Governors of the other presidencies, when the Council shall assemble within their territory.

Additional Members, for the purpose of making Laws and Regulations, nominated under 24 & 25 Vict. c. 67, sec. 10.

Syad Ameer Hossein, C.I.E.

Raja Durga Chara Laha, C.I.E.

Sir G. H. P. Evans, K.C.I.E.

Dr. Rash Behari Ghose

G. R. Elsmie

Deputy Sec., E. N. Baker

Under Sec., R. A. Gamble

Assist. Sec., J. E. O'Connor, C.I.E.

Registrar, W. C. Fink

FOREIGN DEPARTMENT.

Deputy Sec., W. J. Cuningham

Under Sec., J. A. Crawford

LEGISLATIVE DEPARTMENT.

Secretary, S. Harvey James

SECRETARIAT—continued.

Deputy Sec., J. M. Macpherson

K.C.I.E., S.C.

Col. M. J. King-Harman, S.C.

R.E., Major H. F. S. Ramsden, S.C., and Capt.
E. De Brath.

Deputy Acct. Gen., Maj. W. J. B. Bird, S.C.
Asst. Acct. Gen. Capt. W. C. F. Field, S.C.

PUBLIC WORKS DEPARTMENT.

Secretary, F. L. O'Callaghan, C.S.I.
Deputy Secs., Col. F. J. Home, C.S.I., R.E.
(Civil Works, Irrigation and Telegraph
Branch); Lt.-Col. R. A. Sargeant, R.E.
(Railway Branch); Lt.-Col. A. G. Begbie,
R.E.—on furl. (Accounts Branch)
Under Secs., C. Vincent (Civil Works, &c.
Branch); F. B. Hebbert (Railway Branch);
R. G. Macdonald (Accounts Branch); G. H.
D. Walker, B.A. (General Branch).

BENGAL ESTABLISHMENT.

Substantive appointments only are stated. The names of all Civil Servants will also be found in the lists relating to the Provinces and Departments in which they are serving.

MEMO.—The Superintendents of the Central Jails (except Allahabad) are also Superintendents of the District Jails at the same stations. The remaining District Jails are usually in charge of the Civil Surgeons of the several Districts.

The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Delhi Division for the time being, for Dujana

The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Delhi Division for the time being, for Maler Kotla

The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Jullundur Division for the time being, for Kapúrthalla

The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Derájat Division, for the time being, for Frontier Tribes bordering on the districts of his Division

HILL STATES.

Comprising Sirmur (Nahan), Kahlú (Bilaspur), Bashahr, Hindur (Nálagarh), Keonthal, Baghal, Baghat Jubbol, Kumharsain, Bhajji, Mailog, Balsan, Dhama, Kuthar, Kunhiar, Mangal, Bija, Darkoti, Tarhoch and Sängri. The Deputy Commissioner of Simla is ex-officio Superintendent

FRONTIER TRIBES.

The Deputy Commissioners of Hazára, Pesháwúr, Kohát, Bannu, Dera Ismail Khan and Dera Ghazi Khan are Political Officers with respect to the Independent Tribes bordering on their respective Districts

BOMBAY.

TOOK HIS SEAT 12 APRIL, 1890.

GRADATION LIST OF GENERAL AND FIELD OFFICERS OF
THE INDIAN ARMY.

Bengal; M. Madras; Bo. Bombay; C. Cavalry I. Infantry.

NOTE.—The names, in Italics, of Officers of the Staff Corps (who are borne for promotion on a separate List—see Page 195)—are retained upon this list for the purpose of regulating the promotion of Officers of the Indian Army.

BRITISH INFANTRY.The Royal Scots (Lothian Regiment.)(Late 1st Foot.)SECOND BATTALION.

The Collar and Badge of the Order of the Thistle, with the Royal Cypher. The Thistle ensigned with the Imperial Crown. The Sphinx superinscribed "Egypt."

"Blenheim"—"Ramillies"—"Oudenarde"—"Malplaquet"—"Louisburg"—"St. Lucia"—
"Egmont-op-Zee"—"Coronna"—"Busaco"—"Salamanca"—"Vittoria"—
"St. Sebastian"—"Nive"—"Peninsula"—"Niagara"—"Waterloo"—
"Nagpore"—"Maheidpore"—"Ava"—"Alma"—"Inkerman"—
"Sevastopol"—"Taku Forts"—"Pekin."

Scarlet—Facings Blue.

Arrived in India 24th March 1892.—Wellington. Detachments at Calicut,
Cannanore, and Malapuram.

Regimental District No. 1—Glencorse.

Rank and Names. Regimental Rank. Army Rank. Remarks.

BRITISH INFANTRY.The Queen's (Royal West Surrey) Regiment.
(Late 2nd Foot.)SECOND BATTALION.

The Paschal Lamb. The Royal Cypher within the Garter. The Sphinx.

"Pristinæ virtutis memor"—"Vel exuviae triumphant."

"Egypt"—"Vimiera"—"Coronna"—"Salaunanca"—"Vittoria"—"Pyrenees"—"Nivelle"—"Toulouse"—

BRITISH INFANTRY.The Cameronians (Scottish Rifles).(Late 90th Foot.)

SECOND BATTALION.

The Sphinx—The Dragon.

“Blenheim”—“Ramillies”—“Oudenarde”—“Malplaquet”—“Mandora”—“Egypt”—“Corunna”—
“Martinique”—“Guadaloupe”—“China”—“South Africa, 1846-7”—“Sevastopol”—
“Lucknow.”—“Abyssinia”—“South Africa, 1877-8-9.”

The Royal Dublin Fusiliers.
(SECOND BATTALION.)
(103rd Foot.)

The Elephant—The Royal Tiger.
"Spectamur Agendo."

BOMBAY RETIRED OFFICERS.

(The names of General Officers of the Local Service retired under the Royal Warrant of 31st December, 1877, and of those of the Staff Corps and Royal (late Indian) Artillery and Engineers retired under the Royal Warrants of 13th August, 1877, 1st May, 1878, and 25th June, 1881, will be found at pages 193, 199, 203 and 205.)

Agents—Messrs Coutts and Co., Strand.

For information relating to this Fund, application should be made to the Agents.

INDEX.—ACTIVE SERVICE.

NOTE.—Native names will be found under the first portion of the name, omitting the following words when used as titles:—Babu, Bakhshi, Haji, Jemadar, Kazi, Khan Bahadur, Khan Sahib, Khwajah, Maharajah, Maulvi, Mir, Mirza, Nawab, Pundit, Rai, Rai Bahadur, Rajah, Rao, Rao Bahadur, Rao Saheb, Seth, Shahzadah Shaikh, Sirdar, Subadar, Syud, and Wazirzadah.
